channel.title: Open Bankruptcy Project — News & Research
channel.description: Empirical research on US consumer and small-business bankruptcy: filing trends, attorney-fee transparency, district-level statistics, and free PACER alternatives. 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

item.title: Research - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: Learn about research - open bankruptcy project in our free 2026 guide. Clear explanations, real data, and practical steps.

item.title: Free Bankruptcy Case Lookup — Search Public Filings by Name | Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: Free public-records search for federal bankruptcy filings. Type a name; find the case. 8 million federal bankruptcy cases indexed across all 94 U.S. judicial districts. No account, no credit card, no fees. From a 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

item.title: Find Someone in Bankruptcy — Free Public Records Search
item.description: Search 7.9M federal bankruptcy filings by debtor name. Free, public records only — no PACER fee, no signup. Find out if someone has filed Chapter 7, 11, or 13 in any U.S. district.

item.title: Free PACER Alternative — Bankruptcy Court Records Without the Fees
item.description: Free alternative to PACER for federal bankruptcy court records. Search 7.9M case captions, no $0.10/page fee, no signup. Document downloads via the free RECAP Archive where available.

item.title: Bankruptcy Records Lookup — Free Federal Case Search
item.description: Free lookup of federal bankruptcy court records. Search 7.9M case captions by debtor name or case number across all 95+ U.S. bankruptcy districts. No PACER fees, no signup.

item.title: About - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: About the Open Bankruptcy Project: our mission, history, methodology, and leadership.

item.title: Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in W.D. Mo.: 9,393 Cases, 64.8% Dismissal Rate
item.description: Learn about chapter 13 bankruptcy in w.d. mo.: 9,393 cases, 64.8% dismissal rate - open bankruptcy project in our free 2026 guide.

item.title: Chapter 13 Completion Rates Vary Widely Across Federal Districts
item.description: Learn about chapter 13 completion rates vary widely across federal districts - open bankruptcy project in our free 2026 guide.

item.title: Bankruptcy in Missouri: 20,509 Chapter 13 Cases Across 2 Districts
item.description: Learn about bankruptcy in missouri: 20,509 chapter 13 cases across 2 districts - open bankruptcy project in our free 2026 guide.

item.title: Methodology - How We Analyze 8 Million Bankruptcy Cases
item.description: Our research methodology: how the Open Bankruptcy Project collects, cleans, and analyzes 8 million federal bankruptcy cases across 94 districts using open-source tools. Peer-reviewed by academic researchers.

item.title: RECAP Contributions - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: The Open Bankruptcy Project donates federal bankruptcy court documents to the RECAP Archive, making them permanently free to the public. ...

item.title: PACER Reform: Why Court Records Should Be Free
item.description: Why PACER fees should be eliminated. The case for free public access to federal court records, the E-Government Act violation, and reform...

item.title: For Legal Aid Organizations - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: Free, open-source bankruptcy screening tools for legal aid organizations. No fees, no contracts, no client data collection.

item.description: Download free bankruptcy datasets from the Open Bankruptcy Project. 8 million cases, district-level statistics, attorney outcomes, and ...

item.title: BAPCPA at Twenty: What 8 Million Cases Reveal About the ABI Commission's Unfinished Business - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: Learn about bapcpa at twenty: what 8 million cases reveal about the abi commission

item.title: Public-Record Deletion Requests - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: How the Open Bankruptcy Project handles requests to remove individual debtor records, attorney references, or case-index pages. Public-records framework, FRBP 9037 remedies, what OBP can and cannot do.

item.title: Open Bankruptcy Project Launches National Federal Bankruptcy Case Index - Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: The Open Bankruptcy Project has launched a national, district-level index of federal bankruptcy cases covering all 94 U.S. bankruptcy court districts, built from the Free Law Project
